header.title: SNOMED EHR Lab
header.subtitle: A hands-on demo suite showing how to design clinical workflows that produce clean, connected, computable data ready for safety checks, analytics, and exchange. Powered by FHIR and SNOMED CT, but framed around end-to-end information quality.
header.phiWarning: Do not enter Protected Health Information (PHI) or real patient-identifiable information into this demo system.

reasonForEncounter.title: Reason for Encounter
reasonForEncounter.p1: The Reason for Encounter field uses SNOMED CT Expression Constraint Language (ECL) to retrieve all options from a FHIR-Based, SNOMED-CT terminology server.
reasonForEncounter.p2Pre: Reason for Encounter options are retrieved here using
reasonForEncounter.p3: Options are filtered based on the user input and the field is implemented with an auto-complete component to limit user typing.
reasonForEncounter.p4Pre: If the terminology server supports it, returned results can be limited by using
reasonForEncounter.p4Post: . This may prevent the server from responding with a 'too costly' error and improve performance of the lookup and rendering.

diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
diagnosis.p1: The Diagnosis field uses SNOMED CT Expression Constraint Language (ECL) to retrieve all options from a FHIR-Based, SNOMED-CT terminology server.
diagnosis.p2Pre: Diagnosis options are retrieved here using
diagnosis.p3: Options are filtered based on the user input and the field is implemented with an auto-complete component to limit user typing.
diagnosis.p4Pre: If the terminology server supports it, returned results can be limited by using
diagnosis.p4Post: . This may prevent the server from responding with a 'too costly' error and improve performance of the lookup and rendering.

diagnosisNote.title: Diagnosis Note
diagnosisNote.p1: The Diagnosis Note field allows a user to qualify the SNOMED CT concept in the Diagnosis field. In other words, to add more detail to the SNOMED CT concept if necessary.
diagnosisNote.p2: This field is one example of how to deal with a SNOMED CT concept that might not be specific enough. This approach allows the user to choose the closest available SNOMED CT concept and then provide the extra details that are necessary.

procedure.title: Procedure
procedure.p1: The Procedure field uses SNOMED CT Expression Constraint Language (ECL) to retrieve all options from a FHIR-Based, SNOMED-CT terminology server.
procedure.p2Pre: Procedure options are retrieved here using
procedure.p3: Options are filtered based on the user input and the field is implemented with an auto-complete component to limit user typing.
procedure.p4Pre: If the terminology server supports it, returned results can be limited by using
procedure.p4Post: . This may prevent the server from responding with a 'too costly' error and improve performance of the lookup and rendering.
procedure.p5Pre: This field is an example of where you might use the
procedure.p5Post: in a SNOMED CT concept to pre-populate or enable other fields. The Laterality field will pre-populate if you select a concept whose procedure site has been modelled with a laterality (e.g. 735085002 | Excision of left breast lump |).
procedure.p6: The laterality will also become enabled for user input when a concept has a procedure site, but no laterality has been specified (e.g. 392021009 | Lumpectomy of breast |). This will be further refined to restrict enablement to procedure sites in the SNOMED CT lateralisable concept reference set.

laterality.title: Laterality
laterality.p1: The Laterality field uses SNOMED CT Expression Constraint Language (ECL) to retrieve all options from a FHIR-Based, SNOMED-CT terminology server.
laterality.p2Pre: Laterality options are retrieved here using
laterality.p3Pre: This field is an example of a field that is populated based on the
laterality.p3Post: of a concept in another field. The Procedure field controls the enablement and available choices of this Laterality field.
laterality.p4: The Laterality field will pre-populate if you select a procedure concept whose procedure site has been modelled with a laterality (e.g. 735085002 | Excision of left breast lump |).
laterality.p5: The laterality will also become enabled for user input when a procedure concept has a procedure site, but no laterality has been specified (e.g. 392021009 | Lumpectomy of breast |). This will be further refined to restrict enablement to procedure sites in the SNOMED CT lateralisable concept reference set.

info.intro: This demo uses this open source resource:
info.serviceTitle: SNOMED FHIR Clinical Decision Support Service
info.overview: This demo showcases the SNOMED FHIR CDS Service and is intended for demonstration purposes only, not for real-world clinical use. Built on FHIR standards and SNOMED CT, it analyzes medication orders to detect contraindications, drug interactions, abnormal dosing, and validation errors.
info.rulesEngineText: Loads medication-condition and drug-drug interaction rules.
info.fhirIntegrationText: Accepts MedicationRequest, Condition, AllergyIntolerance, and Patient resources.
info.interactionsText: A limited list of medication interaction risks created from publicly available resources for the demo.
info.contraindicationsText: A limited list of medication-condition contraindications created from publicly available resources for the demo.
info.dddText: Uses WHO ATC Defined Daily Doses to estimate excessive dosage alerts.
info.contraindicationAlertsText: Flags when a medication is unsafe for a patient's existing medical conditions. WARNING indicates a critical contraindication, while INFO suggests caution.
info.allergyAlertsText: Flags when a medication conflicts with recorded allergy information.
info.highDosageAlertsText: Identifies when the prescribed daily dose exceeds the WHO Defined Daily Dose reference. INFO indicates 2-4x DDD and WARNING indicates 4x DDD or higher.
info.formulaText: Prescribed Daily Dose / DDD Reference = Alert Threshold
info.configurationText: Acceptable threshold 2x DDD, maximum threshold 4x DDD.
info.drugInteractionAlertsText: Detects risky medication combinations, with INFO, WARNING, and CRITICAL severities based on interaction strength.
info.validationErrorAlertsText: Flags incomplete or invalid prescription data such as invalid dose units, missing frequency, or incomplete information.

fields.indicationReason: Select the reason for the indication

tooltips.infoButton: Click for more information about this field

info.terminologyBindingDesc: This field uses SNOMED CT Expression Constraint Language (ECL) to retrieve options from a FHIR-based SNOMED CT terminology server.
info.eclExpressionIntro: The ECL expression used for this binding is:
info.autocompleteDesc: Options are filtered based on user input and the field is implemented with an auto-complete component to limit user typing and improve the selection experience.
info.countDesc: If the terminology server supports it, returned results can be limited by using count=X. This may prevent the server from responding with a 'too costly' error and improve performance of the lookup and rendering.
info.associationNote: Note: You can associate this medication with an existing condition or procedure by selecting the reason for indication from the dropdown.
